# Browse the OpenAgreements catalog

This generated reference inventories what the repository publishes. Start with
the [quick start](../quickstart.md) if you want to fill an agreement; use this
page when you already know which kind of material you need.

## Find primary-source-backed legal guidance

{{LEGAL_PRACTICE_LIBRARY}}

## Choose an agreement template

The Source column identifies the upstream standard or canonical project page.
The License column describes redistribution terms. A missing Repo link for an
NVCA field-selector means OpenAgreements publishes transformation instructions,
not the proprietary source DOCX.

{{AVAILABLE_TEMPLATES}}

## Review an agreement with a checklist

Here, “checklist” means the substantive, source-anchored review primitive—not a
procedural task tracker. See the [canonical terminology](../concepts/content-and-documents.md#reserve-checklist-for-substantive-review-requirements).

{{CHECKLISTS}}
